overview.summary:
Ship a native Windows x64 release of the DartClaw core runtime: install via PowerShell or Scoop, run `dartclaw serve` with Web UI, FTS5-backed storage/search, a Windows-compatible config reload path, and native Windows turns for both Claude and Codex. Unix-coupled features (container isolation, bash workflow steps without Git Bash) degrade with explicit actionable errors instead of crashes or false security claims.

P1 lands the two independent foundations: a centralized platform capability surface that all later Windows gating routes through, and the Windows build/packaging pipeline with FTS5-guaranteed bundled SQLite. P2 delivers parallel feature slices on top of those foundations: process lifecycle, config reload, degradation paths, harness validation, and the installer. P3 hardens with the repeatable Windows smoke path and synchronizes all documentation with the shipped capability contract.

sharedDecisions.title: Platform capability surface API
sharedDecisions.description: S01 defines the single documented capability surface (home-directory resolution, executable lookup, shell capability, process termination semantics, feature availability); S03, S04, S05, S06, and S07 route all Windows-specific gating and lookups through it instead of adding ad hoc Platform.isWindows checks.

sharedDecisions.title: Unsupported-capability error contract
sharedDecisions.description: S01 defines the structured unsupported-feature/lookup-failure error shape (names the capability, includes attempted context, points at remediation); S04's POSIX-only signal-reload message, S05's container-isolation rejection, and S06's missing-bash step failure all use it; S07 consumes the lookup-failure branch for harness binary resolution.

sharedDecisions.title: Windows artifact naming and bundle layout
sharedDecisions.description: S02 fixes the release asset name `dartclaw-v<version>-windows-x64.zip` (v-prefixed, matching the existing `dartclaw-v${version}-<target>` release convention per the FR4 packaging-unchanged constraint; the PRD's un-prefixed gloss is reconciled in the S02 FIS) and the `build/cli/windows_x64/bundle/{bin,lib}` executable/DLL layout; S08's installer and Scoop manifest and S09's smoke path consume that exact contract.

sharedDecisions.title: Windows config reload mechanism
sharedDecisions.description: S04 selects and documents the supported per-platform reload mechanism (existing file-watch trigger is the leading candidate for Windows; SIGUSR1 stays POSIX-only); S09's smoke coverage and S10's documentation name the same mechanism.

bindingConstraints.verbatim: Windows artifacts must be built on Windows hosts; no macOS/Linux cross-compilation path is available.
Existing macOS/Linux artifacts and packaging behavior remain unchanged.
DartClaw search requires FTS5. Windows must not depend on system `winsqlite3.dll`.
POSIX SIGUSR1 behavior remains available and regression-tested.

Release readiness requires the Windows smoke path to pass. Credential-only CI skips are acceptable only when recorded manual evidence covers the same provider checks for both Claude and Codex, including OS/arch, provider versions, artifact or source under test, and turn results.
Missing bash fails the step explicitly and preserves workflow error reporting; it never returns an empty success result.

stories.name: Platform capability surface
stories.scope: Centralize OS-dependent behavior in one documented platform capability surface covering home-directory resolution, executable lookup, shell availability/choice, process termination semantics, file permission capabilities, and feature availability, including the structured unsupported-capability error contract. Replace direct HOME reads in the Codex environment path and remove direct home-directory/executable-lookup bypasses in touched areas. Excludes consuming-feature behavior changes (lifecycle, reload, degradation), which land in dependent stories.
stories.notes: Foundation story: sharedDecisions 'Platform capability surface API' and 'Unsupported-capability error contract' originate here.

stories.name: Windows release artifact with FTS5-guaranteed SQLite
stories.scope: Build and publish a Windows x64 release asset (`dartclaw-v<version>-windows-x64.zip` containing `dartclaw.exe` plus required DLLs in a loadable layout) from a Windows CI host, using the S0a-proven build path. Neutralize sqlite3 `source: system` at every level affecting the workspace build so Windows loads the bundled FTS5-capable SQLite, with tests asserting ENABLE_FTS5 and the loaded module identity, and pre-publish artifact validation (executable smoke, DLL presence). Existing macOS/Linux build, packaging, and gates remain unchanged. Excludes installer/Scoop distribution (S08).
stories.notes: Merged from FR4 + FR5: both live in the same build/packaging/CI surface; separate specs would drift. S0a spike evidence: `dart build cli` on windows-latest produces the bundle with FTS5 DLL; workspace-root `source: system` override is the known trap.

stories.name: Windows-safe process lifecycle
stories.scope: Make harness and child-process shutdown honest on Windows: harness-pool shutdown must not rely on SIGTERM/SIGKILL semantics, Windows hard-terminate behavior is documented and tested, and managed subprocess cleanup avoids orphans during normal shutdown, with lifecycle warnings when termination cannot be confirmed. Excludes signal-based config reload (S04) and provider-specific harness protocol work (S07).

stories.name: Cross-platform config reload
stories.scope: Provide a supported Windows config reload path while preserving regression-tested POSIX SIGUSR1 reload. Signal-triggered reload reports POSIX-only behavior on Windows via the shared error contract; invalid reload keeps the previous valid config active; documentation names the supported mechanism per platform. Excludes new reload UI surfaces.
stories.notes: A file-watch reload trigger already exists alongside SIGUSR1 (ConfigNotifier/Reconfigurable); the story validates/promotes a Windows-supported path rather than inventing one from scratch.

stories.name: Explicit container-isolation degradation
stories.scope: Mark container isolation unavailable on native Windows: credential-proxy Unix-socket and permission paths must not execute unguarded, and attempts to enable isolation return an actionable unsupported-feature error naming the capability and pointing to POSIX/WSL, with POSIX behavior unaffected. Excludes any Windows-native isolation redesign (out of scope for 0.21).
stories.notes: Security-honesty story: the failure mode being prevented is a false security claim, not a crash.

stories.name: Explicit bash-step degradation
stories.scope: Make workflow bash steps predictable on Windows: run through Git Bash when `bash.exe` is detected (qualified for version capture, cwd, environment propagation, path handling, basic POSIX command execution), and fail with a clear 'bash steps require Git Bash on Windows' error otherwise, never an empty success. POSIX behavior unchanged. Excludes PowerShell/polyglot script semantics (deferred to Workflow DSL v2).

stories.name: Native Windows harness validation
stories.scope: Validate Claude and Codex harness paths on native Windows through the existing harness pool: full DartClaw-managed turns for both providers, CRLF-tolerant line parsing regression coverage, Codex app-server compatibility (camelCase `sandboxPolicy.type`, unknown notifications ignored without crashing), Codex project-trust warnings captured and surfaced, and harness executable resolution routed through the platform capability surface. Excludes provider sandbox parity and the broader runtime smoke path (S09).
stories.notes: S0b spike evidence: native Windows Claude/Codex turns round-trip over stdio emitting LF; Codex compatibility items (sandboxPolicy.type casing, unknown notification tolerance) must become regression tests.

stories.name: Windows installer and Scoop manifest
stories.scope: Provide first-class Windows install paths: an `install.ps1` that downloads the Windows x64 zip, installs `dartclaw.exe` and DLLs together, records a persistent user PATH entry so `dartclaw` resolves in a newly opened terminal, and handles existing installs, upgrades, download/checksum failures, and unsupported architectures without leaving a partial active install; plus a Scoop manifest installing the same asset. Excludes winget/Chocolatey.

stories.name: Windows runtime smoke test
stories.scope: Maintain a repeatable Windows verification path covering server startup, Web UI load, FTS5 search, the Windows-compatible config reload, and Claude/Codex harness turns — runnable in CI where credentials allow, with a documented manual profile otherwise, and evidence output sufficient to diagnose Windows-only failures by layer. Partial failures identify the failed layer and do not report the platform as supported. Excludes new test-framework infrastructure beyond what the smoke path needs.
stories.notes: Existing testing profiles live in dev/testing/; the smoke path should extend that convention rather than invent a parallel one.

stories.name: Windows documentation and capability matrix
stories.scope: Synchronize user, architecture, and planning documentation with the shipped Windows contract: user-guide install/upgrade/smoke/provider-setup pages with a capability/degradation matrix (supported, degraded, unavailable, unverified, remediation), Codex project-trust setup coverage, architecture-doc coverage of the capability surface and Windows constraints, roadmap alignment (Windows = 0.21, Workflow DSL v2 = 0.24, Dynamic Workflows = 0.25) across both repos, Workflow DSL v2 docs treating Git Bash as the Windows baseline, milestone updates to STATE.md/ROADMAP.md/feature-comparison.md, and any ADR for the capability surface or isolation deferral. No page may claim unsupported Windows parity.
stories.notes: Repo policy requires doc updates as explicit stories; this story is the milestone's doc gate and depends on all shipped behavior.

riskSummary.mitigation: Limit bypass removal to touched areas per FR1/US07; POSIX behavior covered by existing test suite plus new dual-platform capability tests.
Reuse the S0a-proven build command and known source-mode trap; pre-publish artifact validation (exe smoke + DLL + FTS5 assertion) blocks bad uploads; macOS/Linux gates must stay green.
Document hard-terminate semantics as the Windows contract instead of emulating signals; verify no-orphan behavior via lifecycle test or smoke evidence.
Fail-closed gating: guard Unix-socket/permission paths before execution and add Windows-unavailable plus POSIX-unaffected tests, so no configuration yields a false isolation claim.
S0b spike already proved the transport path; encode its compatibility findings as regression tests; provider-credential gaps covered by the recorded manual-evidence rule for both providers.
Cover FR6 edge cases (upgrade, PATH write failure, checksum mismatch, unsupported arch) explicitly; installer aborts before activating a partial install.
CI-with-credentials preferred; documented manual profile with recorded evidence for both providers is the accepted fallback per the FR8 binding constraint.

executionNotes: P1's two stories are independent and run in parallel. P2's six slices all depend only on P1 (S08 on S02; the rest on S01) and can run fully parallel. S09 is the integration gate — it needs the artifact, lifecycle, reload, and harness stories done. S10 closes the milestone and must reflect actually-shipped behavior, so it runs last. Windows-host access (CI runner or machine) is required for S02, S07, S08, and S09 verification; provider credentials for S07/S09 may be substituted with the documented manual-evidence profile covering both Claude and Codex. All code work lands in dartclaw-public; this plan and FIS files are private-repo artifacts exported via the implementation bundle when execution starts.
